concepts.title: Imagery in On the Face of It
concepts.description: Imagery in the poem is used to convey the speaker's emotions and thoughts. The use of sensory details such as the 'face like a mask', 'a pair of eyes like two pieces of coal', and 'the lips that I have kissed' creates a vivid image of the speaker's experience.

concepts.title: The poet's attitude towards the speaker's lover
concepts.description: The poet's attitude towards the speaker's lover is one of detachment and objectivity, as evident from the lines 'I look at you with the eyes of a stranger'. This highlights the speaker's inability to see their lover as they truly are.

concepts.title: The theme of alienation in On the Face of It
concepts.description: The poem explores the theme of alienation, where the speaker feels disconnected from their lover and the world around them. This is reflected in lines such as 'I look at you with the eyes of a stranger' and 'I do not see you'.

concepts.title: Key phrases to remember
concepts.description: To remember key phrases, use the acronym: FACE (Feels Alienated, Cannot Express). This can help students recall the speaker's emotions and thoughts throughout the poem.

concepts.title: Misinterpreting the speaker's emotions
concepts.description: Students often misinterpret the speaker's emotions as being romantic or affectionate, but in reality, the speaker is experiencing feelings of alienation and detachment. This can be avoided by paying close attention to the language and imagery used in the poem.

concepts.title: Stanza analysis
concepts.description: To remember the stanzas, use the following units: 1. The speaker's initial emotions (lines 1-4), 2. The speaker's detached attitude (lines 5-8), 3. The speaker's inability to see their lover (lines 9-12).

concepts.title: The use of metaphor in the poem
concepts.description: The poem uses the metaphor of a 'mask' to describe the speaker's lover, highlighting the speaker's perception of their lover as being artificial or fake. This is a key element in understanding the speaker's emotions and thoughts throughout the poem.

concepts.title: The irony in the poem
concepts.description: The poem features irony, as the speaker claims to 'see' their lover, but in reality, they are unable to see them. This highlights the speaker's emotional state and their detachment from reality.
